title: Invalid Element Counting in Stack Implementation

statement.markdown: Implement a basic stack with push, pop, and isEmpty operations. The function should check if the stack is empty and return true or false accordingly.
statement.inputFormat: The program will be tested with multiple inputs. The first line of each input will contain the number of elements to push onto the stack. The next lines will contain the elements themselves.
statement.outputFormat: For each test case, print Yes if the stack is empty and No otherwise.
statement.notes: Time Complexity: O(1) for push and pop operations, O(n) for isEmpty operation

hints.text: Check the isEmpty operation and its return type

editorial.title: Invalid Element Counting in Stack Implementation
editorial.intuition: The isEmpty operation should return true if the stack is empty and false otherwise
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Iterate over the stack to check if it is empty
editorial.betterSolution: Optimize the isEmpty operation to check if the stack is empty in constant time
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a boolean flag to track if the stack is empty
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution correctly implements the isEmpty operation
commonMistakes: Incorrectly returning true for a non-empty stack
Incorrectly returning false for an empty stack

functionSignature.cpp: bool push(int x); int pop(); bool isEmpty();
functionSignature.java: void push(int x); int pop(); boolean isEmpty();
functionSignature.python: def push(self, x: int) -> None; def pop(self) -> int; def isEmpty(self) -> bool;
functionSignature.javascript: function push(x); int pop(); boolean isEmpty();
